services.name: AWS Lambda
services.description: Run code without provisioning or managing servers. Pay only for the compute time you consume.
services.pricing: Pay per request and compute duration

limits.name: Rate of capacity provider write API requests
limits.description: The maximum combined rate (requests per second) for all capacity provider write APIs.

limits.name: Rate of SendDurableExecutionCallbackSuccess API requests
limits.description: The maximum number of SendDurableExecutionCallbackSuccess API requests per second.

limits.name: Rate of GetDurableExecutionHistory API requests
limits.description: The maximum number of GetDurableExecutionHistory API requests per second.

limits.name: Function and layer storage
limits.description: The amount of storage that's available for deployment packages and layer archives in the current Region.

limits.name: Function timeout
limits.description: The maximum timeout that you can configure for a function.

limits.name: Synchronous payload
limits.description: The maximum size of an incoming synchronous invocation request or outgoing response.

limits.name: Rate of GetDurableExecution API requests
limits.description: The maximum number of GetDurableExecution API requests per second.

limits.name: Rate of control plane API requests (excludes invocation, GetFunction, and GetPolicy requests)
limits.description: The maximum number of API requests per second (excluding invocation, GetFunction, and GetPolicy requests).

limits.name: Concurrent executions
limits.description: The maximum number of events that functions can process simultaneously in the current Region.

limits.name: SQS Event Source Mapping throughput in default mode on Lambda Managed Instances
limits.description: The maximum data throughput (MB/s) per SQS event source mapping in default mode on Lambda Managed Instances.

limits.name: Deployment package size (unzipped)
limits.description: The maximum size of the contents of a deployment package or layer archive when it's unzipped.

limits.name: Maximum running durable executions
limits.description: The maximum number of durable executions that can be running simultaneously in the current region.

limits.name: Deployment package size (direct upload)
limits.description: The maximum size of a deployment package or layer archive when you upload it directly to Lambda. Upload larger files with Amazon S3.

limits.name: Capacity providers
limits.description: The maximum number of capacity providers that can be created in an account.

limits.name: Rate of SendDurableExecutionCallbackFailure API requests
limits.description: The maximum number of SendDurableExecutionCallbackFailure API requests per second.

limits.name: Kinesis Event Source Mapping throughput on Lambda Managed Instances
limits.description: The maximum data throughput (MB/s) per Kinesis event source mapping on Lambda Managed Instances.

limits.name: Rate of SendDurableExecutionCallbackHeartbeat API requests
limits.description: The maximum number of SendDurableExecutionCallbackHeartbeat API requests per second.

limits.name: Environment variable size
limits.description: The maximum combined size of environment variables that are configured on a function.

limits.name: Rate of GetFunction API requests
limits.description: The maximum number of GetFunction API requests per second.

limits.name: Rate of CheckpointDurableExecution API requests
limits.description: The maximum number of CheckpointDurableExecution API requests per second.

limits.name: Function versions per capacity provider
limits.description: The maximum number of function versions per capacity provider.

limits.name: Maximum number of durable operations per durable execution
limits.description: The maximum number of durable operations (including customer-initiated operations and automatic retries) that trigger state persistence within a single durable execution.

limits.name: Function invocation rate to initiate durable executions
limits.description: The maximum number of new durable executions that you can initiate per second.

limits.name: Rate of ListDurableExecutionsByFunction API requests
limits.description: The maximum number of ListDurableExecutionsByFunction API requests per second.

limits.name: Rate of GetDurableExecutionState API requests
limits.description: The maximum number of GetDurableExecutionState API requests per second.

limits.name: Kafka Event Source Mappings in default mode on Lambda Managed Instances
limits.description: The maximum number of Kafka event source mappings in default mode on Lambda Managed Instances.

limits.name: Processes and threads
limits.description: The maximum combined number of processes and threads that a function can have open.

limits.name: Asynchronous invocation request throughput on Lambda Managed Instances
limits.description: The maximum invoke request throughput (MB/s) for asynchronous invocations on Lambda Managed Instances. Asynchronous invocations occur when Lambda functions are invoked by services such as Amazon S3 or Amazon SNS.

limits.name: Rate of capacity provider read API requests
limits.description: The maximum combined rate (requests per second) for all capacity provider read APIs.

limits.name: File descriptors
limits.description: The maximum number of file descriptors that a function can have open.

limits.name: Kafka Event Source Mapping throughput in default mode on Lambda Managed Instances
limits.description: The maximum data throughput (MB/s) per Kafka event source mapping in default mode on Lambda Managed Instances.

limits.name: Rate of GetPolicy API requests
limits.description: The maximum number of GetPolicy API requests per second.

limits.name: Function layers
limits.description: The maximum number of layers that you can add to your function.

limits.name: DynamoDB Event Source Mapping throughput on Lambda Managed Instances
limits.description: The maximum data throughput (MB/s) per DynamoDB event source mapping on Lambda Managed Instances.

limits.name: Durable execution storage written in megabytes
limits.description: The maximum cumulative amount of data persisted per durable execution, including input and output payloads, checkpoints and error data, measured in megabytes.

limits.name: Asynchronous payload
limits.description: The maximum size of an incoming asynchronous invocation request.

limits.name: Function resource-based policy
limits.description: The maximum combined size of resource-based policies that are configured on a function.

limits.name: Deployment package size (console editor)
limits.description: The maximum size of a deployment package or layer archive when you upload it through the console editor. Upload larger files with Amazon S3.

limits.name: Test events (console editor)
limits.description: The maximum amount of test events for a function through the console editor.

limits.name: Concurrency scaling rate
limits.description: The maximum immediate increase in function concurrency that can occur in response to an increase in traffic or execution duration. Every 10 seconds, each synchronously invoked Lambda function can scale by an additional 1,000 concurrent executions until the total concurrency across your functions in a Region reaches the account concurrency limit.

limits.name: Rate of StopDurableExecution API requests
limits.description: The maximum number of StopDurableExecution API requests per second.

limits.name: Storage for uploaded functions (.zip file archives) and layers
Storage for functions defined as container images
Elastic network interfaces per virtual private cloud (VPC) This quota is shared with other services, such as Amazon Elastic File System (Amazon EFS)
Function memory allocation
limits.value: 128 MB to 10,240 MB, in 1-MB increments. Note: Lambda allocates CPU power in proportion to the amount of memory configured. You can increase or decrease the memory and CPU power allocated to your function using the Memory (MB) setting. At 1,769 MB, a function has the equivalent of one vCPU.

limits.name: Function environment variables
limits.value: 4 KB, for all environment variables associated with the function, in aggregate

limits.name: Function concurrency scaling limit
limits.value: For each function, 1,000 execution environments every 10 seconds

limits.name: Invocation payload (request and response)
limits.value: 6 MB each for request and response (synchronous) 200 MB for each streamed response (synchronous) 1 MB (asynchronous) 1 MB for the total combined size of request line and header values

limits.name: Bandwidth for streamed responses
limits.value: Uncapped for the first 6 MB of your function's response For responses larger than 6 MB, 2MBps for the remainder of the response

limits.name: Deployment package (.zip file archive) size
limits.value: 50 MB (zipped, when uploaded through the Lambda API or SDKs). Upload larger files with Amazon S3. 50 MB (when uploaded through the Lambda console) 250 MB The maximum size of the contents of a deployment package, including layers and custom runtimes. (unzipped)

limits.name: Container image code package size
limits.value: 10 GB (maximum uncompressed image size, including all layers)

limits.name: /tmp directory storage
limits.value: Between 512 MB and 10,240 MB, in 1-MB increments

limits.name: Execution processes/threads
limits.value: 1,024 Lambda Managed Instances use the default process and thread limits from Bottlerocket. For more information, see Understanding the Lambda Managed Instances execution environment.

limits.name: Invocation requests per function per Region (synchronous)
limits.value: Each instance of your execution environment can serve up to 10 requests per second. In other words, the total invocation limit is 10 times your concurrency limit. See Understanding Lambda function scaling.

limits.name: Invocation requests per function per Region (asynchronous)
limits.value: Each instance of your execution environment can serve an unlimited number of requests. In other words, the total invocation limit is based only on concurrency available to your function. See Understanding Lambda function scaling.

limits.name: Invocation requests per function version or alias (requests per second)
limits.value: 10 x allocated provisioned concurrency This quota applies only to functions that use provisioned concurrency.

limits.name: GetFunction API requests
limits.value: 100 requests per second. Cannot be increased.

limits.name: GetPolicy API requests
limits.value: 15 requests per second. Cannot be increased.

limits.name: Remainder of the control plane API requests (excludes invocation, GetFunction, and GetPolicy requests)
limits.value: 15 requests per second across all APIs (not 15 requests per second per API). Cannot be increased.

news.title: AWS Lambda functions can now mount Amazon S3 buckets as file systems with S3 Files
AWS Lambda Durable Execution SDK for Java GA
AWS Lambda expands response streaming support to all commercial AWS Regions

services.name: Amazon DynamoDB
services.description: Fast, flexible NoSQL database service for single-digit millisecond performance at any scale.
services.pricing: Pay per read/write capacity unit or on-demand

limits.name: Global Secondary Indexes per table
limits.description: The maximum number of global secondary indexes that can be created for a table. For more information, see, https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazondynamodb/latest/developerguide/ServiceQuotas.html#limits-secondary-indexes

limits.name: Table-level write throughput limit
limits.description: The maximum number of write throughput allocated for a table or global secondary index. For more information, see https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazondynamodb/latest/developerguide/Limits.html#default-limits-throughput-capacity-modes

limits.name: Write throughput limit for DynamoDB Streams (Provisioned mode)
limits.description: The maximum number of write capacity units allowed for a table with streams enabled; applicable only for tables in provisioned read/write capacity mode. Other quotas might also apply. For more information, see https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazondynamodb/latest/developerguide/ServiceQuotas.html#limits-dynamodb-streams

limits.name: Account-level write throughput limit (Provisioned mode)
limits.description: The maximum number of write capacity units allocated for the account; applicable only for tables (including all associated global secondary indexes) in provisioned read/write capacity mode. For more information, see https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazondynamodb/latest/developerguide/Limits.html#default-limits-throughput-capacity-modes

limits.name: Maximum number of tables
limits.description: The maximum number of tables that can be created per region. For more information, see https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazondynamodb/latest/developerguide/ServiceQuotas.html#limits-tables

limits.name: Table-level read throughput limit
limits.description: The maximum number of read throughput allocated for a table or global secondary index. For more information, see https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazondynamodb/latest/developerguide/Limits.html#default-limits-throughput-capacity-modes

limits.name: Concurrent control plane operations
limits.description: The maximum number of allowed concurrent control plane operations. For more information, see https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazondynamodb/latest/developerguide/Limits.html#limits-api

limits.name: Account-level read throughput limit (Provisioned mode)
limits.description: The maximum number of read capacity units allocated for the account; applicable only for tables (including all associated global secondary indexes) in provisioned read/write capacity mode. For more information, see https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazondynamodb/latest/developerguide/Limits.html#default-limits-throughput-capacity-modes

limits.name: Provisioned capacity decreases per day
limits.description: A decrease is allowed up to four times any time per day (GMT time zone). Also, if there was no decrease in the past hour, an additional decrease is allowed, effectively bringing the maximum number of decreases in a day to 27 times. For more information, see https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazondynamodb/latest/developerguide/ServiceQuotas.html

services.name: Amazon SQS
services.description: Fully managed message queuing service to decouple and scale microservices.

limits.name: Attributes per Message
limits.description: The number of attributes added to a message.

limits.name: Tags per Queue
limits.description: The number of tags added to a queue.

limits.name: UTF-8 Queue Tag Value Length
limits.description: The length of a UTF-8 queue tag value.

limits.name: Conditions per Queue Policy
limits.description: The number of conditions in a queue policy.

limits.name: Messages per Batch
limits.description: The number of messages in a message batch.

limits.name: Statements per Queue Policy
limits.description: The number of statements in a queue policy.

limits.name: Principals per Queue Policy
limits.description: The number of principals in a queue policy.

limits.name: Queue Name Length
limits.description: The queue name length.

limits.name: UTF-8 Queue Tag Key Length
limits.description: The length of a UTF-8 queue tag key.

limits.name: Message Size
limits.description: The size of a message, in kilobytes.

limits.name: Message Retention Time
limits.description: The length of time, in seconds, for which Amazon SQS retains a message if it isn't deleted. The maximum is 14 days (1,209,600 seconds).

limits.name: Message Size in S3 Bucket
limits.description: The size of a message, in gigabytes, in an Amazon S3 bucket.

limits.name: Batched Message ID Length
limits.description: The length of a batched message ID.

limits.name: Message Invisibility Period
limits.description: The length of time, in seconds, for which a message consumed from a queue remains invisible to other consumers.

limits.name: Actions per Queue Policy
limits.description: The number of actions in a queue policy.

limits.name: Queue Policy Size
limits.description: The size, in bytes, of a queue policy.

limits.name: In-Flight Messages per Standard Queue
limits.description: The number of in-flight messages in a standard queue.

limits.name: Queue Delivery Delay
limits.description: The length of time, in minutes, by which to delay the initial delivery of messages to a queue.

services.name: Amazon API Gateway
services.description: Create, publish, maintain, monitor, and secure APIs at any scale.
services.pricing: Pay per API call and data transfer

limits.name: Domain name access associations
limits.description: The maximum number of domain name access associations that you can create in this account in the current region

limits.name: Regional APIs
limits.description: The maximum number of regional APIs that you can create in this account in the current region

limits.name: Maximum Iterations In Mapping Template
limits.description: Maximum number of iterations in a #foreach ... #end loop in mapping templates

limits.name: Throttle rate
limits.description: The maximum number of requests per second that your APIs can receive in this account in the current region

limits.name: Maximum Logo Size
limits.description: The maximum size of a logo

limits.name: PortalProducts per account
limits.description: The maximum number of PortalProducts per account

limits.name: Maximum Custom Domain Names per Portal
limits.description: The maximum number of custom domain names per portal

limits.name: Resources/Routes per REST/WebSocket API
limits.description: The maximum number of resources/routes that you can include in a REST or WebSocket API

limits.name: WebSocket message payload size
limits.description: Maximum WebSocket message payload size.

limits.name: Edge-optimized APIs
limits.description: The maximum number of edge-optimized APIs that you can create in this account in the current region

limits.name: Maximum Authorizers per Portal
limits.description: The maximum number of authorizer per portal

limits.name: Routes per HTTP API
limits.description: The maximum number of routes that you can include in an HTTP API

limits.name: Portals per account
limits.description: The maximum number of Portals per account

limits.name: WebSocket new connections burst rate
limits.description: New connections in burst capacity per account (across all WebSocket APIs) per region

limits.name: Stage variables per stage
limits.description: Stage variables per stage

limits.name: WebSocket frame size
limits.description: Maximum WebSocket frame size.

limits.name: ProductPages per PortalProduct
limits.description: The maximum number of ProductPages per PortalProduct

limits.name: API keys
limits.description: The maximum number of API keys that you can create in this account in the current region

limits.name: Tags Per Stage
limits.description: Maximum tags per stage.

limits.name: VPC links(V2)
limits.description: The maximum number of V2 VPC links that you can create in this account in the current Region

limits.name: Edge API URL Length
limits.description: Length, in characters, of the URL for an edge-optimized API.

limits.name: Stages per API
limits.description: The maximum number of stages that you can create for an API

limits.name: WebSocket new connections rate
limits.description: New connections per second per account (across all WebSocket APIs) per region

limits.name: Maximum Combined Header Size
limits.description: Maximum combined size of all header values

limits.name: Maximum Size per ProductPage body
limits.description: The maximum size per ProductPage body

limits.name: Regional API URL Length
limits.description: Length, in characters, of the URL for a regional API

limits.name: ProductRestEndpointPages per PortalProduct
limits.description: The maximum number of ProductRestEndpointPages per PortalProduct

limits.name: Custom Domain Names
limits.description: The maximum number of Custom domain names that you can create in this account in the current region

limits.name: PortalProducts per Portal
limits.description: The maximum number of PortalProducts per Portal

limits.name: Usage plans
limits.description: The maximum number of usage plans that you can create in this account in the current region

limits.name: RoutingRules Per Domain Name
limits.description: The maximum number of RoutingRules per DomainName that you can create in this account in the current region

limits.name: Stage Variable Key Length
limits.description: Length, in characters, of the key in a stage variable

limits.name: AWS Lambda authorizer result size
limits.description: The maximum size of AWS Lambda authorizer result.

limits.name: VPC links
limits.description: The maximum number of VPC links that you can create in this account in the current region

limits.name: Subnets per VPC link(V2)
limits.description: The maximum number of subnets per V2 VPC link in this account in the current Region

limits.name: Maximum Documentation Size per ProductRestEndpointPage
limits.description: The maximum documentation size per ProductRestEndpointPage

limits.name: Stage Variable Value Length
limits.description: Length, in characters, of the value in a stage variable

limits.name: Maximum Cached Response Size
limits.description: Maximum size of cached response in bytes

limits.name: Usage plans per API key
limits.description: The maximum number of usage plans that you can associate with an API key

limits.name: Private custom domain names
limits.description: The maximum number of private custom domain names that you can create in this account in the current region

limits.name: Throttle burst rate
limits.description: The maximum number of additional requests per second (RPS) that you can send in one burst in this account in the current region

limits.name: WebSocket Idle Connection Timeout
limits.description: WebSocket API idle connection timeout.

limits.name: Method ARN Length
limits.description: ARN length of a method with authorization

limits.name: Maximum integration timeout in milliseconds
limits.description: The maximum integration timeout (in milliseconds) allowed for your account in the current region. This limit can only be increased for Regional and private APIs.

limits.name: Maximum API caching TTL
limits.description: The maximum API caching TTL you can have in this account in the current region.

limits.name: API Payload Size
limits.description: Maximum payload size for non WebSocket API.

limits.name: Maximum resource policy size in bytes
limits.description: The maximum resource policy size in bytes you can have in this account in the current region.

limits.name: Private APIs
limits.description: The maximum number of private APIs that you can create in this account in the current region

limits.name: Client certificates
limits.description: The maximum number of certificates that you can associate with this account in the current region

limits.name: Connection duration for WebSocket API
limits.description: Maximum duration for WebSocket API connection.

limits.name: API Stage throttles in a usage plan
limits.description: The maximum number of API-stage throttle settings you can create in a usage plan in this account in the current region

limits.name: Throttle quota per account, per Region across HTTP APIs, REST APIs, WebSocket APIs, and WebSocket callback APIs
limits.value: 10,000 requests per second (RPS) with an additional burst capacity provided by the token bucket algorithm, using a maximum bucket capacity of 5,000 requests. \$1 The burst quota is determined by the API Gateway service team based on the overall RPS quota for the account in the Region. It is not a quota that a customer can control or request changes to.

limits.name: Throttle quota without access control per account per Region for a portal
Throttle quota with access control per account per Region for a portal
CreateRestApi for Regional or private API
ImportRestApi for Regional or private API
Other operations
limits.value: No quota up to the total account quota.

limits.name: Total operations
limits.value: 10 requests per second with a burst quota of 40 requests per second.

limits.name: Public custom domain names per account per Region
Private custom domain names per account per Region
Private APIs per account per Region

Length, in characters, of the value in a stage variable

VPC links per account per Region
API caching TTL
limits.value: 300 seconds by default and configurable between 0 and 3600 by an API owner.

limits.name: Cached response size
limits.value: 1048576 Bytes. Cache data encryption may increase the size of the item that is being cached.

limits.name: Integration timeout for Regional APIs
limits.value: 50 milliseconds - 29 seconds for all integration types, including Lambda, Lambda proxy, HTTP, HTTP proxy, and AWS integrations.

limits.name: Integration timeout for edge-optimized APIs
limits.value: 50 milliseconds - 29 seconds for all integration types, including Lambda, Lambda proxy, HTTP, HTTP proxy, and AWS integrations.

limits.name: Integration timeout for private APIs
limits.value: 50 milliseconds - 29 seconds for all integration types, including Lambda, Lambda proxy, HTTP, HTTP proxy, and AWS integrations.

limits.name: Total combined size of all header values, including header names, values, any line terminators, and whitespaces
Total combined size of all header values for a private API
Method-level throttling settings for a stage in a usage plan
Number of certificates in a truststore

Total combined size of request line and header values

Response size from JSON Web Key Set endpoint

services.name: Amazon S3
services.description: Object storage built to retrieve any amount of data from anywhere.
services.pricing: Pay per GB stored and requests

limits.name: General purpose buckets
limits.description: The number of Amazon S3 general purpose buckets that you can create in an account

limits.name: Replication rules
limits.description: The maximum number of rules you can specify in an Amazon S3 Replication configuration

limits.name: Replication Destinations
limits.description: The number of destination S3 buckets you can add for each source S3 bucket across all replication rules.

limits.name: Replication transfer rate
limits.description: The maximum Replication Time Control transfer rate that you can replicate from the source region in this account.

limits.name: Object size
limits.description: The maximum size (in TB) of an Amazon S3 object

news.title: AWS Lambda functions can now mount Amazon S3 buckets as file systems with S3 Files
Amazon S3 Express One Zone now supports S3 Inventory
Amazon S3 Lifecycle pauses actions on objects that are unable to replicate

services.name: AWS AppSync
services.description: Managed GraphQL service to build data-driven apps with real-time and offline capabilities.
services.pricing: Pay per query and real-time update

limits.name: GraphQL APIs - Source API associations per Merged API
limits.description: The maximum number of Source API associations per Merged API.

limits.name: Event APIs - Number of characters allowed in a channel segment
limits.description: The maximum character length of a channel segment

limits.name: Event APIs - APIs per region
limits.description: The maximum number of AppSync Event APIs per region

limits.name: GraphQL APIs - Rate of request tokens
limits.description: The maximum number of request tokens per second for GraphQL APIs in this account in the current Region. AWS AppSync allocates tokens to mutation and query requests based on the amount of resources (processing time and memory) that they consume. For more details on tokens, see the Monitoring section in the AWS AppSync documentation.

limits.name: Event APIs - Rate of connections per API
limits.description: The maximum number of Event API connect requests per second.

limits.name: Event APIs - Number of segments allowed in a channel
limits.description: The maximum number of segments in a channel

limits.name: All APIs - Subscriptions per client connection
limits.description: Subscriptions code statements per client in a single WebSocket connection. Each statement allows the client to receive multiple messages related to the specific subscription.

limits.name: GraphQL APIs - Rate of inbound messages per API
limits.description: The maximum number of inbound messages per second. Each subscription type invoked by a mutation generates one inbound message.

limits.name: GraphQL APIs - APIs per region
limits.description: Maximum number of GraphQL APIs per region

limits.name: All APIs - Number of custom domain names
limits.description: The maximum number of custom domain names per region

limits.name: Event APIs - Batch size per publish request
limits.description: The maximum number of events that can be batched in one publish operation.

limits.name: Event APIs - Rate of outbound messages per API
limits.description: The maximum number of outbound metered events per second. One metered event equals 5 kB of delivered event.

limits.name: GraphQL APIs - Rate of connections per API
limits.description: The maximum number of GraphQL API connect requests per second.

limits.name: Event APIs - Publish payload size
limits.description: The maximum size of the entire payload sent in a publish operation

limits.name: Event APIs - Channel namespaces per API
limits.description: The maximum number of channel namespaces on an Event API

limits.name: GraphQL APIs - Maximum query length
limits.description: Maximum length of a GraphQL query, measured in tokens (15,000). Tokens are individual parsed elements of the query string. Queries exceeding this limit will fail with the error message 'Maximum char length exceeded'.

limits.name: Event APIs - Rate of request tokens
limits.description: The maximum number of request tokens per second for Event APIs in this account in the current Region. AWS AppSync allocates tokens to publish requests based on the amount of resources (processing time and memory) that they consume and the number of 5kB messages published.

limits.name: GraphQL APIs - Rate of outbound messages per API
limits.description: The maximum number of outbound messages per second. One outbound message is equal to 5 KB of payload data delivered.

limits.name: Event APIs - Rate of inbound events per API
limits.description: The maximum number of inbound events per second. Each published event generates a single inbound event.

limits.name: All APIs - Handler, resolver, and function code size
limits.description: Maximum code size of handlers, resolvers, and functions running on APPSYNC_JS runtime.

limits.name: Event APIs - Rate of publish requests per client connection
limits.description: The maximum number of publish requests per second per WebSocket connection. WebSocket publish requests are also evaluated against the rate of request tokens quota.
